title: eriogonum pharnaceoides
intro: Eriogonum pharnaceoides, also known as Mormon tea, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Eriogonum pharnaceoides include its ability to lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of arthritis, as well as its potential to reduce the risk of certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of Eriogonum pharnaceoides can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat a variety of health conditions due to its medicinal properties.
